title: Build backend floor is too low for SPDX-style license metadata
reasoning: The project declares only setuptools>=68 as its build backend, but uses the string form of project.license. That form relies on newer SPDX-style project metadata support; older setuptools releases allowed by the declared floor may reject or mishandle it during isolated builds, making source/wheel builds depend on whatever backend version pip happens to resolve.
recommendation: Either raise the build-system setuptools minimum to a version that supports string license expressions, or use the legacy PEP 621 table form compatible with the declared backend floor.
whyTestsDoNotAlreadyCoverThis: No packaging/build test is listed for this feature, and the README test command only runs pytest after an editable install rather than exercising isolated sdist/wheel metadata generation.
suggestedRegressionTest: Add a packaging smoke test in CI that runs `python -m build` or equivalent isolated wheel/sdist build from a clean environment.

history.reasoning: The original evidence path still exists. In the current working tree, pyproject.toml lines 1-3 set the build backend requirement to setuptools>=77.0.3, while line 11 still uses license = MIT. git diff confirms the previous setuptools>=68 floor has been raised. A packaging smoke test in a temp Python 3.12 venv pinned to setuptools==77.0.3 built both sdist and wheel successfully, and the generated wheel metadata contains Metadata-Version 2.4 and License-Expression: MIT. This directly addresses the reported minimum-backend risk.
commands: tmpdir="$(mktemp -d)"; /opt/homebrew/bin/python3.12 -m venv "$tmpdir/venv"; "$tmpdir/venv/bin/python" -m pip install -q "setuptools==77.0.3" "build" "wheel"; "$tmpdir/venv/bin/python" -m build --no-isolation --sdist --wheel --outdir "$tmpdir/dist" /Users/ewi/repos/mint; ls -la "$tmpdir/dist"
